strengths: 17 years of documented software engineering experience (2009–2026) exceeding the 15-year minimum, across full-stack web, infrastructure, DevOps, and systems handling millions of requests per second

Current hands-on AI infrastructure work at re:cinq (MCP code generators, agentic frameworks) paired with proven ability to ship at scale (Apple TV app ranked top-10 US; micro-services handling real-time data)
Track record of technical leadership across multiple Staff and Founder roles, with demonstrated ability to inspire through workshops, conference talks, and open-source platform (GISCollective)
concerns: No Master's or PhD in AI/ML—résumé lists only BS Computer Science (MIT); advanced AI education is strongly preferred for a Principal-level AI strategy role and is notably absent

Experience centers on AI infrastructure tooling and application-level development rather than strategic cloud/AI platform building or shipped customer-facing AI products; recent MCP work is tooling, not platform strategy

Limited evidence of cloud platform architecture or strategic vision for product differentiation; background shows application-level cloud usage (AWS CDK, Kubernetes deployment) but not platform-level design or multi-cloud strategy work
response.justification: Szabo exceeds the 15-year minimum with 17 years of documented experience and holds a BS from MIT. His current role at re:cinq (AI-agent infrastructure, MCP frameworks) is timely and shows recent engagement with AI tooling. However, this role demands Principal-level strategic expertise in cloud and AI platforms—not infrastructure engineering. The résumé demonstrates strong execution and recent AI infrastructure work, but lacks evidence of building shipped AI products, designing cloud platform strategy for customer differentiation, or the Master's/PhD in AI that is strongly preferred for director-level hiring. Background is primarily application and infrastructure engineering; the gap between that and director-level AI/cloud platform strategy is substantial.
